title: Fix admin panel permissions error when loading registrations

requirements.text: Fix the admin panel so that after successful Internet Identity login and password authentication, the authenticated identity is correctly passed to the backend actor when fetching bookings and session registrations, eliminating the 'account may not have admin permissions' error.

quotes: Failed to load registrations. Your account may not have admin permissions yet. Please wait a moment and try again.

acceptanceCriteria: After completing both authentication steps (Internet Identity + password), the admin panel successfully loads all bookings without showing a permissions error.
After completing both authentication steps, the admin panel successfully loads all session registrations without showing a permissions error.
The authenticated actor used for admin queries carries the logged-in Internet Identity principal, not an anonymous principal.
Retrying does not produce the permissions error once the user is fully authenticated.
The backend isAdmin check correctly recognises the authenticated caller's principal.

requirements.text: Ensure the useQueries hooks for fetching bookings and registrations wait for the authenticated actor (not the anonymous actor) to be ready before executing the queries, and invalidate/refetch those queries when the actor transitions from anonymous to authenticated.
acceptanceCriteria: Queries for admin data are disabled until the authenticated actor is available.
Once the actor becomes authenticated, the admin queries are automatically triggered without requiring a manual retry.
No permissions error is shown while the actor is still in its unauthenticated/transitional state.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts directly — propose logic changes only through safe alternatives (e.g., AdminPanel.tsx or useQueries.ts).
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file.
nonGoals: Adding new admin features or UI pages.
Changing the two-step authentication flow (Internet Identity + password gate).
Modifying the booking or registration data schema.
